name: cytoolz (Cython-Accelerated Functional Utilities)

performance.notes: Cython-compiled; 2-5x faster than toolz pure Python equivalent

agent_readiness.notes: Cython-compiled version of toolz functional utilities. from cytoolz import curry, compose, pipe, juxt, groupby, reduceby, frequencies, merge, merge_with, valmap, keymap, itemmap, take, drop, partition, sliding_window, interleave, concat. pipe(data, func1, func2, func3). compose(f, g, h)(x). curry(add)(1)(2). groupby(key_fn, iterable). frequencies(['a', 'b', 'a']) → {'a': 2, 'b': 1}. merge({'a': 1}, {'b': 2}). valmap(str.upper, {'a': 'hello'}). partition(2, range(10)). Same API as toolz — drop-in Cython replacement. cytoolz.curried submodule for pre-curried functions. Used by Dask internally.

gotchas: No type annotations — same limitation as toolz
Cython extension — requires compiled binary; not pure Python
API identical to toolz — not beneficial to import both
Some edge cases differ from Python implementation due to C-level behavior
Less readable code in heavily curried pipelines — not for everyone
best_when: Performance-critical data processing pipelines using toolz patterns
When toolz is already a dependency and a drop-in speed boost is needed
Functional programming patterns in Python with speed requirements
Dask users — cytoolz is already a Dask dependency
avoid_when: When toolz pure Python speed is sufficient
Environments without C compilation support (some cloud functions)
When toolz.curried syntax is confusing for the team
summary: cytoolz is the Cython-compiled version of toolz. Drop-in replacement: pipe(), compose(), curry(), groupby(), frequencies(), merge(), valmap(). 2-5x faster than toolz. BSD-3 by PyToolz. Best when toolz is needed for performance-critical code. Same API as toolz — no type annotations. Requires compiled Cython binary. Used internally by Dask. cytoolz.curried for pre-curried functions.
